title: Farming Basics
quickAnswer: Basic farms work best when you respect growth requirements: hydrated farmland for crops, proper spacing and light, valid breeding food for animals, and enough room overhead for tree growth.
keyFacts: Most crop farms need light and nearby water, while automated growth often depends on villager harvesting or redstone collection.
Animal breeding uses species-specific foods, so wheat, carrots, potatoes, seeds, and hay each matter for different mobs.
Simple starter farms usually beat complicated automation until you have stable iron, redstone, and villager support.
commonMistakes: Building compact farms that block light or tree growth space.
Trying to automate too early when a manual renewable farm would solve the immediate resource problem.
